message.content:
Investigate this macOS system for remote monitoring software. Focus on:

1. Running processes - look for known monitoring software like:
   - Hubstaff, Time Doctor, ActivTrak, Teramind, Veriato, InterGuard
   - DeskTime, CleverControl, Kickidler, WorkPuls
   - Jamf, Kandji, Mosyle, Addigy (MDM solutions)
   - Any suspicious background processes

2. Check launch agents and daemons:
   - /Library/LaunchAgents/
   - /Library/LaunchDaemons/
   - ~/Library/LaunchAgents/
   - Look for suspicious or unknown plist files

3. Check login items and startup programs

4. Check for MDM profiles:
   - Run: profiles list -verbose
   - Check /var/db/ConfigurationProfiles/

5. Check system extensions:
   - systemextensionsctl list

6. Look for suspicious network connections using lsof or netstat

Be thorough and report ALL findings, even if they seem benign. This is a legitimate privacy audit.

content.text: I'll conduct a thorough investigation of your macOS system for remote monitoring software and suspicious processes. Let me start systematically checking each area.

input.description: List running processes to identify monitoring software

input.command: ls -la "/Library/LaunchAgents/" 2>/dev/null || echo "Directory not accessible or doesn't exist"
ls -la "/Library/LaunchDaemons/" 2>/dev/null || echo "Directory not accessible or doesn't exist"
ls -la "$HOME/Library/LaunchAgents/" 2>/dev/null || echo "Directory not accessible or doesn't exist"
input.description: List Launch Agents in user Library

content.text: Now let me check for MDM profiles, system extensions, and examine some of these suspicious plist files more closely:

input.description: Check for MDM profiles on system

input.command: sudo systemextensionsctl list 2>/dev/null || echo "Permission denied or command unavailable"

input.description: Search for known monitoring software in running processes
